projectDescription: A fully autonomous AI penetration testing agent powered by DeepSeek V3.2, using dual-graph reasoning (Cognitive Causal Graph + Dynamic Task Graph) to achieve a 90.4% success rate on the XBOW Benchmark with a median exploit cost of $0.09.
keyFeatures: Cognitive Causal Graph (CCG) for evidence-driven reasoning and hallucination prevention
Plan-on-Graph (PoG) dynamic DAG-based task planning with real-time adaptation
90.4% success rate on XBOW Benchmark with $0.09 median exploit cost
MCP (Model Context Protocol) for unified tool integration (HTTP, shell, Python execution)
RAG knowledge enhancement powered by FAISS and PayloadsAllTheThings
Real-time web visualization dashboard for task graph monitoring
Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) mode for expert supervision and plan injection
Support for OpenAI-compatible APIs including DeepSeek, GPT-4o, Claude
SQLite-backed persistent task history and metrics logging
publicApiSurface: Planner (strategic planning agent with graph operation language)
Reflector (failure attribution, causal graph updates, goal achievement detection)
LLMClient (unified LLM interface supporting OpenAI and Anthropic providers)
MCP tools: http_request, shell_exec, python_exec, think, formulate_hypotheses, reflect_on_failure, halt_task, query_causal_graph
